text.title: Resource Management Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the resource-management methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; the final rule defines when to skip this methodology entirely.

rule.statement: Planned utilisation MUST cap at 70% per person; 100% planning leaves zero slack and guarantees slippage.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Each task MUST list required skills; assignment to person without skill flagged as gap.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Resource levelling MUST start from critical-path tasks; non-critical can be deferred without schedule impact.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Actuals (hours logged) MUST be compared to plan weekly; >20% delta triggers replan.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Roster MUST live in git (or equivalent versioned store), not in PM head; loss of PM = loss of plan otherwise.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Skip for single-team work with no shared resources or no roster.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.
